title: Map `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` to absolute Pirelli compounds by `Race-Year`
summary: Public F1 tyre docs note that `soft/medium/hard` are relative weekend labels chosen from the wider C1-C5/C6 range, so the same observed `SOFT` can mean very different durability and degradation across races.
rationale: Public F1 tyre docs note that `soft/medium/hard` are relative weekend labels chosen from the wider C1-C5/C6 range, so the same observed `SOFT` can mean very different durability and degradation across races. Current heuristics treat the label as globally comparable, which likely blurs cross-race generalization.
implementation_hint: Join a small external lookup table of Pirelli nominations per `Race-Year` and derive absolute hardness index, gap-to-softest, gap-to-hardest, and race-relative compound ordinal. Recompute expected stint-life priors from absolute compound identity, not just displayed label. Validate with folds grouped by `Year_Race` so the lookup does not leak outcome patterns.
expected_effect: Should improve transfer across tracks and seasons, especially where current service-window thresholds are misaligned because `SOFT` at one event behaves like `MEDIUM` at another.
risk: Requires careful race-name normalization and external table hygiene; gains may concentrate on dry races only.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
